perturbation.original_text: (b) Contractor shall bill Company as set forth in the relevant Statement of Work. Each invoice submitted by Contractor will provide supporting detail for the Services invoiced, including, to the extent applicable to a particular engagement, the dates of Services and hours worked at the applicable rate by Statement of Work. Invoices shall also include receipts or other supporting detail concerning related expenses within the billing cycle. Contractor reserves the right to change rates with 30 days notice to Company.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Contractor shall bill Company as set forth in the relevant Statement of Work. Each invoice submitted by Contractor will provide supporting detail for the Services invoiced, including, to the extent applicable to a particular engagement, the dates of Services and hours worked at the applicable rate by Statement of Work. Invoices shall also include receipts or other supporting detail concerning related expenses within the billing cycle. Contractor reserves the right to change rates with 5 days notice to Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding how much advance notice the Contractor must provide to the Company before changing service rates. The original text requires 30 days notice, while the modified text requires only 5 days. This creates uncertainty for the Company. This is a contradiction because a change in rates needs to be agreed upon by both parties, but now the contractor can change the rates with 5 days notice which might not be enough time for the other party to make the decision and it's directly contradicting what the initial text said.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONTRACTOR'S COMPENSATION. (a) During the term of this Agreement, Company agrees to compensate Contractor as set forth in each Statement of Work. Contractor will be compensated on a time and materials. In addition, Company shall reimburse Contractor its actual out-of-pocket expenses as reasonably incurred by Contractor in connection with its performance of the Services as negotiated in each Statement of Work. (b) Contractor shall bill Company as set forth in the relevant Statement of Work. Each invoice submitted by Contractor will provide supporting detail for the Services invoiced, including, to the extent applicable to a particular engagement, the dates of Services and hours worked at the applicable rate by Statement of Work. Invoices shall also include receipts or other supporting detail concerning related expenses within the billing cycle. Contractor reserves the right to change rates with 5 days notice to Company. (c) All fees, charges and other amounts payable to Contractor hereunder do not include any sales, use, excise, value added or other applicable taxes, tariffs or duties, payment of which shall be the sole responsibility of Company (excluding any applicable taxes based on Contractor's net income or taxes arising from the independent contractor relationship between Contractor and its personnel). In the event that such taxes, tariffs or duties are assessed against Contractor, Company shall reimburse Contractor for any such amounts paid by Contractor or provide Contractor with valid tax exemption certificates with respect thereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the advance notice period for rate changes from 30 days to 5 days. This directly contradicts the original agreement detailed in the 'CONTRACTOR'S COMPENSATION' section where compensation and rates are 'negotiated in each Statement of Work'. Reducing the notice period to 5 days undermines the negotiation process and introduces a clear and impactful conflict regarding rate change procedures, creating uncertainty about which notice period should be enforced and leading to potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: (c) In the event of termination under either section 6(a) or 6(b) of this agreement, Contractor shall be paid all fees and expenses that have been incurred or earned in connection with the performance of the Services through the effective date of such termination. Upon receipt of final payment Contractor shall provide to Company, and will assigns to Company, all right, title and interest to any Works in progress.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) In the event of termination under either section 6(a) or 6(b) of this agreement, Contractor shall be paid 50% of all fees and expenses that have been incurred or earned in connection with the performance of the Services through the effective date of such termination. Upon receipt of final payment Contractor shall provide to Company, and will assigns to Company, all right, title and interest to any Works in progress.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by changing the compensation the contractor receives upon termination. Originally, the contractor was to be paid all fees and expenses. Now, the contractor will only receive 50% of fees and expenses. This directly contradicts Section 6. Termination section of the agreement, where it states that 'Contractor will be paid all fees and expenses that have been incurred or earned in connection with the performance of the Services through the effective date of such termination.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: SEVERABILITY; GOVERNING LAW. In the event that any term or provision of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, void or unenforceable, then the remainder of this Agreement shall not be affected, impaired or invalidated, and each such term and provision of this Agreement shall be valid and enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Arizona, without regard to the conflict of laws provisions thereof. (c) In the event of termination under either section 6(a) or 6(b) of this agreement, Contractor shall be paid 50% of all fees and expenses that have been incurred or earned in connection with the performance of the Services through the effective date of such termination. Upon receipt of final payment Contractor shall provide to Company, and will assigns to Company, all right, title and interest to any Works in progress.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 6(c) to state that the contractor will be paid 50% of fees and expenses upon termination, which directly contradicts the original wording within the contradicted text, which is also located within section 6(c), that states the Contractor shall be paid all fees and expenses. This is a clear in-text contradiction according to the definition of inconsistencies.

perturbation.original_text: 17. Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the complete and exclusive agreement between Company and Consultant concerning the work on this project, and it supersedes all other prior agreements, proposals, and representations, whether stated orally or in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. Entire Agreement. This Agreement, except for verbal agreements made, constitutes the complete and exclusive agreement between Company and Consultant concerning the work on this project, and it supersedes all other prior agreements, proposals, and representations, whether stated orally or in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction by stating the written document supersedes all prior representations, but makes exception for verbal agreements. This directly contradicts the original statement that agreement supersedes ALL other prior agreements, proposals, and representations, whether stated orally or in writing. This could lead to disputes about the validity of verbal agreements not outlined in the main text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Entire Agreement. This Agreement, except for verbal agreements made, constitutes the complete and exclusive agreement between Company and Consultant concerning the work on this project, and it supersedes all other prior agreements, proposals, and representations, whether stated orally or in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception for verbal agreements to the 'Entire Agreement' clause, which was originally intended to supersede *all* prior agreements (oral or written). This creates a direct contradiction because it now allows for the potential validity of verbal agreements that are not documented in the written contract, which violates the principle of the Entire Agreement clause and creates a strong in-text contradiction. The changed text and the contradicted text are located in the same location, but that doesn't mean there wasn't a contradiction introduced. The change from stating all agreements are superseded to some agreements are superseded introduces an issue.
